summary_en: Sansan authorized repurchase of up to 2M ordinary shares (1.58% of outstanding) for ¥2.0B via market purchase from May 20 to June 30, 2026, alongside first dividend of ¥2.50/share.
title_en: Notice Regarding Modification of Dividend Forecast (Initial Dividend) and Decision on Matters Relating to Acquisition of Treasury Stock
translation_en_brief:
On May 19, 2026, Sansan's board of directors approved both a modification to its dividend forecast and authorization to repurchase treasury stock. This is Sansan's first-ever dividend announcement: ¥2.50 per share payable at fiscal year-end (May 2026). The company justified the inaugural dividend and concurrent buyback by noting strong operational performance, upward revision of FY2027 adjusted operating margin guidance to 20–23%, and achievement of structural profit acceleration. Management believes the business has entered a phase of accelerated profit growth, permitting shareholder distributions while maintaining adequate resources for operations and growth investment.

The buyback authorization permits acquisition of up to 2.0 million ordinary shares (1.58% of outstanding shares, excluding treasury holdings of 142,622 shares as of February 28, 2026) for a maximum aggregate consideration of ¥2.0 billion. Purchases will be executed via market buy on the Tokyo Stock Exchange between May 20 and June 30, 2026. The rationale emphasizes that current share price does not adequately reflect the company's medium-to-long-term enterprise value, particularly given ongoing investments in generative AI and adjacent technologies. Buyback is positioned as the primary near-term vehicle for capital returns, to be optimally combined with dividend policy going forward.
